name: FARADAY FUTURE INTELLIGENT ELECTRIC INC.
business: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. is a California-based technology company focused on the design, engineering, and development of intelligent, connected electric vehicles and related artificial intelligence-enabled technologies. The Company's primary business activities center on its AIEV platform, including the FF and FX vehicle series. The Company also holds a majority interest in AIxCrypto Holdings, Inc., an independent public company whose business is focused on embodied AI infrastructure and blockchain infrastructure.

exposures.sentence: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (the Company) is a California-based technology company focused on the design, engineering, and development of intelligent, connected electric vehicles and related artificial intelligence-enabled technologies.
During 2025, the Company began evaluating a robotics initiative as a potential extension of its broader embodied AI and intelligent mobility strategy.

The FF 91 series features a dual systems-on-a-chip (SoC) computing platform for in-vehicle infotainment, an NVIDIA-based autonomous driving hardware platform, and a high-speed connectivity system capable of supporting up to three simultaneous 5G connections.
Through AIXC, the Company has established a digital asset-related initiative focused on evaluating blockchain-based technologies and investment opportunities.

catalysts.event: First FX Super One pre-production vehicle roll-off
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, the first FX Super One pre-production vehicle rolled off the line at the Company's FF aiFactory California manufacturing facility.

catalysts.event: Initial FX Super One deliveries expected to begin in Q2 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company has outlined a phased delivery strategy for the FX Super One in the United States, with initial deliveries expected to begin in the second quarter of 2026, followed by a gradual ramp-up toward broader consumer availability in late 2026 or early 2027, subject to regulatory approvals, production readiness, and market conditions.
